title: six6 FalkorDB Memory Node V2
description: One final FalkorDB Memory node produced by the ingestion executor from an approved-decision.v2 record. id is the database identity; candidate_id is retained only for audit, traceability, and idempotent upsert lookup.

id.description: Final FalkorDB Memory.id generated by ingestion. It must be stable for one canonical memory node and must not directly reuse candidate_id.

candidate_id.description: Original candidate-layer identifier retained for audit, approved-decision lookup, and repeated-ingestion upsert matching. It is not the final Memory.id.

topic.description: Approved memory title mapped from approved-decision.v2.topic.

content.description: Approved distilled memory content mapped from approved-decision.v2.content.

timestamp.description: Source event time mapped from approved-decision.v2.timestamp.

source.description: Logical source path or process name mapped from approved-decision.v2.source.

source_file.description: Optional physical source file copied from approved-decision.v2.source_file when present in the node export.

source_section.description: Optional source heading copied from approved-decision.v2.source_section when present in the node export.

approved_at.description: Optional human approval time copied from approved-decision.v2.approved_at for node export audit.

ingested_at.description: Optional ingestion executor write time. This field may change on repeated ingestion.

$comment: Generation and idempotency rule: first lookup Memory by candidate_id. If a node exists, preserve id and candidate_id, then update mutable fields from the approved-decision.v2 record. If no node exists, generate id as 'memnode-<YYMMDD>-<sha256("memory-node.v2\n" + candidate_id)[0:16]>'. The uniqueness boundary is one Memory node per candidate_id inside one FalkorDB graph.
